---
title: "Summer 2025 Wildfires in Europe: Where Did It Burn?"
subtitle: "EFFIS burn-scar perimeters (June–August) with monthly facets and country totals"
author: "Pierre Beaucoral"
date: today
format:
  html:
    theme:
     light: minty
     dark: darkly
    respect-user-color-scheme: true
    self-contained: true
    highlight-style: tango
    number-sections: true
    code-fold: true          # per-chunk folding
    code-summary: "Show code"
    code-tools: true         # adds “Show All Code / Hide All Code” toolbar    lightbox: true        # click images to zoom
    toc: true             # optional: show floating table of contents
    toc-depth: 3
    smooth-scroll: true
    link-external-newwindow: true
    df-print: paged
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
filters:
  - quarto
---

::: callout-note
**Method at a glance.** We map **burnt-area perimeters** from EFFIS (Sentinel-2/Landsat based). Small fires below \~**30–50 ha** are under-represented, so totals are **conservative**.
:::

## Hero map — all summer 2025

*Translucent red polygons show the burn scars detected June–August 2025.*

## Monthly facets

*Seasonality emerges: early-summer Iberian/Med focus vs. late-summer Balkans/SE Europe (illustrative).*

## Top-10 countries by burned area (Summer 2025)

```{r}
1+1
```

*Totals by country (sum of scar perimeters’ areas).*

## Δ vs earlier summers

We compare **2025** against **2017** (a milder baseline) and show a multi-year view for the **Top-10** 2025 countries.

*The markers connect each country’s 2017 and 2025 totals; the grouped bars show 2017/2022/2023/2025.*

## (Optional) Severity overlay

*If a severity raster is available, it adds a sense of impact beyond area alone.*

## Data & caveats

-   **Data:** EFFIS Burnt Areas perimeters (Copernicus/JRC).\
-   **Coverage:** Rapid mapping focuses on larger fires; **small fires (\<\~30–50 ha)** may be missed.\
-   **Projection:** All maps in **EPSG:3035 (ETRS89 / LAEA Europe)**.\
-   **Reproducibility:** Re-run the script on publication day to capture the latest perimeters.
